channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Chief Justice John Roberts defends judicial independence, says it is under threat in several ways
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Chief Justice John Roberts issued a defense Tuesday of judicial independence, which he said is under threat from intimidation, disinformation and the prospect of public officials defying court orders. Roberts laid out his concerns in his annual report on the federal judiciary.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Here are 5 hot-button laws taking effect in 2025
item.description: States across the country are bracing for new laws set to take effect as early as Wednesday, including some that have stirred controversy.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Multiple people injured in balloon-drop incident at Massachusetts arcade
item.description: Cheers of excitement quickly changed to horror when the netted balloons pulled a large Lego display down onto the crowd below, including multiple children, Storyful reports.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KRON4

item.title: WATCH LIVE: Stream the NYC Times Square New Year’s Eve Ball Drop
item.description: Watch live streaming coverage of the annual Times Square Ball Drop in New York City on New Year’s Eve!
item.dc:creator: Ken Haddad, Local 4

item.title: PlayStations for guns are offered up in New Orleans
item.description: The city of New Orleans and local community groups are offering PlayStations to people willing to turn in guns.
item.dc:creator: Jack Brook, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: US, Mexico agree to split cost of plugging sinkholes, other repairs at Amistad Dam
item.description: The United States and Mexico have agreed to share costs to repair dozens of sinkholes at an international dam in South Texas.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: German chancellor takes veiled swipe at Elon Musk in New Year's address
item.description: Scholz's comments come days after the German government accused the billionaire of trying to influence its February election.
item.dc:creator: Steff Danielle Thomas, Fox 5

item.title: Georgia judge takes own life in courtroom
item.description: SPRINGFIELD, Ga. (WSAV) – An Effingham County state court judge died by suicide Tuesday, sources told reporters at WSAV. WSAV was told Judge Stephen Yekel took his own life inside of a courtroom. Yekel served as state court judge since 2022, and Tuesday would have been his last day on the bench.
item.dc:creator: Molly Curley, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Strange laws in Nevada, including ones preventing state lotteries and swearing on the Strip
item.description: Nevada is full of laws that may seem rather bizarre. The Silver State's strangest laws include ones against lotteries, swearing and hula-hooping.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump tests influence over GOP with Johnson Speakership vote
item.description: The strength of President-elect Trump’s influence over the razor-thin House Republican majority will get another major test Friday, when Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) aims to be reelected as Speaker.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: A new year dawns on a Middle East torn by conflict and change
item.description: DAMASCUS (AP) — In Damascus, the streets were buzzing with excitement Tuesday as Syrians welcomed in a new year that seemed to many to bring a promise of a brighter future after the unexpected fall of Bashar Assad’s government weeks earlier.
item.dc:creator: GHAITH ALSAYED, WAFAA SHURAFA and LUJAIN JO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Mother of abandoned baby surrenders to police in the Bronx: NYPD
item.description: A 26-year-old woman is facing multiple charges in connection with a baby that was left in a tote bag.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Jack, NewsNation

item.title: Watch the 2025 Rose Parade streaming live
item.description: LA's Very Own KTLA 5 is proud to offer live, nonstop coverage of the 136th Rose Parade presented by Honda on New Year’s Day, Jan. 1, 2025. Our broadcast and streaming coverage begins at 4 a.m. with an encore presentation of the 2024 Tournament of Roses Parade with grand marshal Audra McDonald.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, NewsNation

item.title: US Treasury says Chinese hackers remotely accessed workstations, documents in 'major' cyber incident
item.description: The department said it was working with the FBI and the Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency and others to investigate the impact of the hack, and that the hack had been attributed to Chinese state-sponsored culprits.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: American family shot on Mexico vacation in attack that left 3 dead, teen fighting for life: reports
item.description: A Chicago teenager vacationing in Mexico is in a coma after he, his father and his uncle became targets of a shooting last week. The father and uncle were killed in the attack.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Woman burned to death in horrific subway attack identified: police
item.description: The woman who was heinously burned alive in a subway car last week has been identified as a 57-year-old woman from New Jersey, Fox News Digital has learned.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Have any New Year's resolutions? Here are some tips to help make them stick
item.description: Studies have shown that up to 70% of people who make New Year’s resolutions abandon those good intentions within months. Here are some tips for how to keep those resolutions when your willpower starts to falter.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Delphi murders: Judge rules autopsy photos, records be sealed
item.description: Judge Fran Gull ruled that all crime scene and autopsy photos - as well as medical and mental health records - be sealed.
item.dc:creator: Matt Adams, NewsNation

item.title: Carter helped shape modern day border policies, former aide says
item.description: He described how Carter treated everyone with respect, but "not the kind of guy you could pull a fast one on, he was very smart and a very tough boss.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: New Menendez brothers prosecutor slams 'meritless' conflict claim in push to take case out of Los Angeles
item.description: Los Angeles DA Nathan Hochman's office is pushing back against a claim of a conflict of interest in the Menendez brothers’ case as their supporters seek outside help.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jimmy Carter sought to expand democracy worldwide long after he left the White House
item.description: HARARE, Zimbabwe (AP) — Amid everything else on his desk — the Iran hostage crisis, domestic economic turmoil, the Soviet invasion of Afghanistan and a grueling 1980 reelection fight — President Jimmy Carter elevated the independence of a country in southern Africa as a top agenda item.
item.dc:creator: FARAI MUTSAKA and CHRISTINA A. CASSIDY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Six shot in 'brazen and heartless attack' in the Bronx: NYPD
item.description: THE BRONX, N.Y. (PIX11) -- Six people, including a 12-year-old girl, were shot Monday afternoon in the Bronx, according to the NYPD. The incident happened around 4:50 p.m. at 3544 White Plains Road, police said.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, NewsNation

item.title: Jimmy Carter made eradicating Guinea worm disease a top mission
item.description: JARWENG, South Sudan (AP) — Nobel Prize-winning peacemaker Jimmy Carter spent nearly four decades waging war to eliminate an ancient parasite plaguing the world’s poorest people.
item.dc:creator: RUSS BYNUM and SAM MEDNICK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: NYPD hunting for suspect after baby abandoned in bag on NYC street
item.description: Police are searching for a person they say left an infant girl in a tote bag Sunday morning in the Bronx.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 20 cheese factory workers injured after chemical spill at New Mexico factory releases toxic gas: police
item.description: New Mexico authorities say 20 people were injured after a chemical spill at the Southwest Cheese factory produced toxic gas, incapacitating employees.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Florida woman arrested after allegedly repeatedly stabbing boyfriend's dog: Deputies
item.description: A Florida woman has been charged with animal cruelty after she allegedly stabbed her boyfriend's pit bull because it was "bothering" her, according to deputies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Migrant crisis added to record-level US homelessness: HUD
item.description: Sanctuary cities saw homelessness rates jump thanks in part to a surge of migrants being sent from the southern border.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Appeals court upholds verdict finding Trump liable for sexually assaulting E. Jean Carroll
item.description: A federal appeals court panel on Monday upheld a jury’s verdict finding President-elect Donald Trump liable for sexually assaulting advice columnist E. Jean Carroll and ordering him to pay $5 million.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Argentine judge charges 5 people over death of former One Direction star Liam Payne
item.description: BUENOS AIRES, Argentina (AP) — An Argentine judge has confirmed charges against five people in connection with the death of Liam Payne, a former member of musical group One Direction. The judge has ordered preventive prison for two of them for having supplied Payne with drugs.
item.dc:creator: ALMUDENA CALATRAVA Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Jimmy Carter, the 39th US president, has died at 100
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Jimmy Carter, the peanut farmer who won the presidency in the wake of the Watergate scandal and Vietnam War, endured humbling defeat after one tumultuous term and then redefined life after the White House as a global humanitarian, has died. He was 100 years old.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW and ALEX SANZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Darnold gives Vikings another gem with career-high 377 yards in 27-25 win over Packers
item.description: The Minnesota Vikings were ready to start their postgame ritual in honor of a ninth consecutive victory before realizing Sam Darnold was still missing from the raucous locker room because he was finishing his interview with the TV broadcasting crew.
item.dc:creator: DAVE CAMPBELL, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: US children are struggling with reading
item.description: Learning to read can become exponentially more difficult for older students and adults who have fallen behind, creating grave concerns as the number of individuals struggling with literacy into high school and adulthood is increasing.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KTLA 5

item.title: US teacher jailed in Russia is formally designated as wrongfully detained
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An American schoolteacher arrested in Russia on drug charges more than three years ago has been designated by the U.S. government as wrongfully detained, the State Department said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4
